generatedSentence: The neurotic goalkeeper, largely genial but uxorious, saw his remote campsite as a haven, leaving it only for unilateral hijinks. He'd salvaged old jamjars and washers, oiled for an infra-red system, using untied wires to deploy an ultra-secure perimeter. His ebullient desire for a joyride and kakapo hunts was upfront, yet he felt an imposter, his most fervent dreams censored. This peace was short-lived; a bizarre juggernaut, inexorable and ominous, seemed to shuffle nearby. The inhabitant, a robot of intense violet hue, was applying strange etching tools, its work causing a hairline fracture in the earth, and an arcing flicker of energy. Our hero, learning its gambit, felt the painful truth of a decade's worth of misunderstanding, his chivalry clashing with its malign intent. The robot, nonetheless, kowtows to no one, its hammers striking the root system of an ancient tree, its endoscope revealing endangered tunnels beneath. A hard, yellow lemon, a child's toy, became a dart in its rotations, igniting nearby fires. The resultant chaos drained the vibrant landscape, leaving a wound that seemed impossible to heal, a heartbreaking turnover of nature into slimes. Healed by an ardent sense of romance, our hero, piggybacking on trains, offered shawarma to the weary humans in a nearby hangout, whose household items had been hocked. The milkman, once a sycophant, now joined in the hilarious skiffle band, their music echoing the hammam's warmth. The robot, its lechery forgotten, was now a mere etching, its last act a nickel raffled for homework, a final, corny effort salvaged from the wreck.
panelSentences: The neurotic goalkeeper, largely genial but uxorious, saw his remote campsite as a haven, leaving it only for unilateral hijinks. He'd salvaged old jamjars and washers, oiled for an infra-red system, using untied wires to deploy an ultra-secure perimeter. His ebullient desire for a joyride and kakapo hunts was upfront, yet he felt an imposter, his most fervent dreams censored.

This peace was short-lived; a bizarre juggernaut, inexorable and ominous, seemed to shuffle nearby. The inhabitant, a robot of intense violet hue, was applying strange etching tools, its work causing a hairline fracture in the earth, and an arcing flicker of energy. Our hero, learning its gambit, felt the painful truth of a decade's worth of misunderstanding, his chivalry clashing with its malign intent.

The robot, nonetheless, kowtows to no one, its hammers striking the root system of an ancient tree, its endoscope revealing endangered tunnels beneath. A hard, yellow lemon, a child's toy, became a dart in its rotations, igniting nearby fires. The resultant chaos drained the vibrant landscape, leaving a wound that seemed impossible to heal, a heartbreaking turnover of nature into slimes.

Healed by an ardent sense of romance, our hero, piggybacking on trains, offered shawarma to the weary humans in a nearby hangout, whose household items had been hocked. The milkman, once a sycophant, now joined in the hilarious skiffle band, their music echoing the hammam's warmth. The robot, its lechery forgotten, was now a mere etching, its last act a nickel raffled for homework, a final, corny effort salvaged from the wreck.

He'd salvaged old jamjars and washers, oiled for an infra-red system, using untied wires to deploy an ultra-secure perimeter. His ebullient desire for a joyride and kakapo hunts was upfront, yet he felt an imposter, his most fervent dreams censored.

This peace was short-lived; a bizarre juggernaut, inexorable and ominous, seemed to shuffle nearby. The inhabitant, a robot of intense violet hue, was applying strange etching tools, its work causing a hairline fracture in the earth, and an arcing flicker of energy. Our hero, learning its gambit, felt the painful truth of a decade's worth of misunderstanding, his chivalry clashing with its malign intent.

The robot, nonetheless, kowtows to no one, its hammers striking the root system of an ancient tree, its endoscope revealing endangered tunnels beneath. Healed by an ardent sense of romance, our hero, piggybacking on trains, offered shawarma to the weary humans in a nearby hangout, whose household items had been hocked. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: wearing a lime-green cape and a silver chrome helmet, a retro-futuristic backpack. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist meticulously connecting infra-red wires to old jamjars at a high-tech campsite, looking intently at a glowing screen, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: An ominous, multi-limbed robot juggernaut with violet glowing eyes, etching a hairline fracture into the ground with glowing tools, extreme wide shot, night. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, wearing his helmet, dodging a lemon-yellow dart arcing from the robot towards a flaming ancient tree, dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, cape billowing, offering shawarma to a group of grateful humans and a musician playing a skiffle instrument in a neon-lit, salvaged hangout, eye-level shot.

definitions.definition: To make high demands on (a system or service).
To declare (a person) a defaulter on the stock exchange.
To beat down the price of (a stock), or depress (a market).
To have hard sex with
definitions.example: Daniel hammered Mary til he came.

definitions.definition: To make better from a disease, wound, etc.; to revive or cure.
definitions.example: This bandage will heal your cut.

definitions.definition: To become better or healthy again.
definitions.example: Bandages allow cuts to heal.

definitions.definition: To reconcile, as a breach or difference; to make whole; to free from guilt.
definitions.example: to heal dissensions

definitions.definition: To hide, conceal, and keep secret, especially for a secret society (such as the masons).
(now especially in the phrase "hele in") To cover or conceal (a seedling, plant, roots, etc).
The act of kowtowing.

To cut into a surface with an acid or other corrosive substance in order to make a pattern. Best known as a technique for creating printing plates, but also used for decoration on metal, and, in modern industry, to make circuit boards.
To engrave a surface.
To make a lasting impression.
definitions.example: The memory of 9/11 is etched into my mind.

definitions.definition: To sketch; to delineate.
The art of producing an image from a metal plate into which an image or text has been etched with acid.
The image created by this process.
An instrument used to examine a bodily orifice or canal, or a hollow organ.
To put (someone or something) in danger; to risk causing harm to.
To incur the hazard of; to risk; to run the risk of.
In danger, at risk, said of something where there is a strong possibility something bad will happen to it (for example, a species in danger of going extinct)
Full of ardor; fervent, passionate.
Burning; glowing; shining.
Elongated portion.
Connected sequence of people or things.
To practice an ability.
definitions.example: She trained seven hours a day to prepare for the Olympics.

definitions.definition: To teach and form (someone) by practice; to educate (someone).
definitions.example: You can't train a pig to write poetry.

definitions.definition: To improve one's fitness.
definitions.example: I trained with weights all winter.

definitions.definition: To proceed in sequence.
To move (a gun) laterally so that it points in a different direction.
definitions.example: The assassin had trained his gun on the minister.

definitions.definition: To encourage (a plant or branch) to grow in a particular direction or shape, usually by pruning and bending.
definitions.example: The vine had been trained over the pergola.

definitions.definition: To trace (a lode or any mineral appearance) to its head.
To create a trainer for; to apply cheats to (a game).
To draw along; to trail; to drag.
To draw by persuasion, artifice, or the like; to attract by stratagem; to entice; to allure.
Treachery; deceit.
A trick or stratagem.
A trap for animals; a snare.
A lure; a decoy.
The amount of money taken as sales transacted in a given period.

definitions.definition: The frequency with which stock is replaced after being used or sold, workers leave and are replaced, a property changes hands, etc.
definitions.example: High staff-turnover can lead to low morale amongst employees

definitions.definition: A semicircular pastry made by turning one half of a circular crust over the other, enclosing the filling (usually fruit).
definitions.example: They only served me one apple turnover for breakfast.

definitions.definition: A loss of possession of the ball without scoring.
definitions.example: The Nimrods committed another dismaying turnover en route to another humiliating loss.

definitions.definition: A measure of leg speed: the frequency with which one takes strides when running, typically given in strides per minute.
The act or result of overturning something; an upset.

definitions.definition: An apprentice, in any trade, who is handed over from one master to another to complete his time.
Capable of being turned over; designed to be turned over.

definitions.definition: A yellowish citrus fruit.
A semitropical evergreen tree, Citrus limon, that bears such fruits.
A taste or flavour/flavor of lemons.
A more or less bright shade of yellow associated with lemon fruits.
A defective or inadequate item or individual.
definitions.example: He didn’t realise until he’d paid for it that the car was a lemon.

definitions.definition: (shortened from “lemon flavour”) Favor.
definitions.example: A thousand quid for that motor? Do me a lemon, I could get it for half that.

definitions.definition: A piece of fanfiction involving explicit sex (named after the erotic anime series Cream Lemon).
To flavour with lemon.
definitions.example: You can start the vegetables cooking while you are lemoning the fish.

definitions.definition: Containing or having the flavour/flavor and/or scent of lemons.
Of the pale yellow colour/color of lemons.
(from "lemon tart") Smart; cheeky, vocal.
A confection made from a paste of almonds, sugar and egg white as a binder.
To cover with marzipan.
definitions.example: a marzipanned cake

definitions.definition: To appear; to look outwardly; to be perceived as.
definitions.example: He seems to be ill.   Her eyes seem blue.   It seems like it is going to rain later.   How did she seem to you?

definitions.definition: To befit; to beseem.
To pass lightly; to glide along in an even, smooth course; to glide along near the surface.
To pass near the surface of; to brush the surface of; to glide swiftly along the surface of.
To hasten along with superficial attention.
To put on a finishing coat of plaster.
To throw an object so it bounces on water (skimming stones)
To ricochet
To read quickly, skipping some detail

definitions.definition: To scrape off; to remove (something) from a surface
To clear (a liquid) from scum or substance floating or lying on it, by means of a utensil that passes just beneath the surface.

definitions.definition: To clear a liquid from (scum or substance floating or lying on it), especially the cream that floats on top of fresh milk

definitions.definition: To steal money from a business before the transaction has been recorded, thus avoiding detection.
To surreptitiously scan a payment card in order to obtain its information for fraudulent purposes.
To become coated over.
A pointed missile weapon, intended to be thrown by the hand, for example a short lance or javelin
Any sharp-pointed missile weapon, such as an arrow.
(sometimes figurative) Anything resembling such a missile; something that pierces or wounds like such a weapon.
A small object with a pointed tip at one end and feathers at the other, which is thrown at a target in the game of darts.
A dart-shaped target towed behind an aircraft to train shooters.
A plan or scheme.
A sudden or fast movement.
A fold that is stitched on a garment.
A fish, the dace.
A cigarette.
A group, set, or series of ten , particularly:

definitions.definition: A set of resistors, capacitors, etc. connected so as to provide even increments between one and ten times a base electrical resistance.
The interval between any two quantities having a ratio of 10 to 1.

definitions.definition: To lose liquid.
definitions.example: The clogged sink drained slowly.

definitions.definition: To flow gradually.
definitions.example: The water of low ground drains off.

definitions.definition: To cause liquid to flow out of.
definitions.example: Please drain the sink. It's full of dirty water.

definitions.definition: To convert a perennially wet place into a dry one.

definitions.definition: To deplete of energy or resources.

definitions.definition: To draw off by degrees; to cause to flow gradually out or off; hence, to exhaust.
To filter.
To fall off the bottom of the playfield.
Lacking motivation and energy; very tired; knackered.

definitions.definition: Of a battery, empty of charge; discharged.
An injury, such as a cut, stab, or tear, to a (usually external) part of the body.
A hurt to a person's feelings, reputation, prospects, etc.

definitions.definition: An injury to a person by which the skin is divided or its continuity broken.
To hurt or injure (someone) by cutting, piercing, or tearing the skin.

definitions.definition: To hurt (a person's feelings).
definitions.example: The actor's pride was wounded when the leading role went to his rival.

definitions.definition: To destroy violently; to cause severe damage to something, to a point where it no longer works, or is useless.

definitions.definition: To ruin or dilapidate.
To dismantle wrecked vehicles or other objects, to reclaim any useful parts.
To involve in a wreck; hence, to cause to suffer ruin; to balk of success, and bring disaster on.
Destroyed, usually in an accident; damaged to the point of unusability.
Very intoxicated from alcohol and/or other drugs.
Having been put in a dreadful or embarrassing situation; can range from being pwned in a game to being utterly defeated in an argument or publicly shamed with a stinging insult.
A silvery elemental metal with an atomic number of 28 and symbol Ni.
A coin worth 5 cents.
(by extension) Five dollars.
(by extension) Five hundred dollars.
(sometimes the nickel or the hot nickel) Interstate 5, a highway that runs along the west coast of the United States.
A playing card with the rank of five
A five-year prison sentence.
A defensive formation with five defensive backs, one of whom is a nickelback, instead of the more common four.
To plate with nickel.
Adjacent, near, close by

definitions.definition: Pulsing with energy or activity.

definitions.definition: Lively and vigorous.
Vibrating, resonant or resounding.
(of a colour) Bright.
To lose remembrance of.

definitions.definition: Euphemism for fuck, screw (a mild oath).

definitions.definition: An unsteady flash of light.

definitions.definition: A short moment.
To burn or shine unsteadily, or with a wavering light.
To keep going on and off; to appear and disappear for short moments; to flutter.
To flutter; to flap the wings without flying.
A story relating to chivalry; a story involving knights, heroes, adventures, quests, etc.
An intimate relationship between two people; a love affair.
A strong obsession or attachment for something or someone.
Idealized love which is pure or beautiful.

An embellished account of something; an idealized lie.
An adventure, or series of extraordinary events, resembling those narrated in romances.

definitions.definition: A dreamy, imaginative habit of mind; a disposition to ignore what is real.

To woo; to court.

definitions.definition: Having the edge finely notched.
The act of turning around a centre or an axis.

definitions.definition: A single complete cycle around a centre or an axis.

definitions.definition: A regular variation in a sequence, such as to even-out wear, or people taking turns in a task; a duty roster.

definitions.definition: An operation on a metric space that is a continuous isometry and fixes at least one point.

definitions.definition: The set of starting pitchers of a team.
The step during takeoff when the pilot commands the vehicle to lift the nose wheel off the ground during the takeoff roll. (see also: V2)

Stressful and tiring.
Very severe.
